seoTitle: Create a Fillable Form in Google Docs Using Tables
metaDescription: Build a fillable intake form in Google Docs with tables, dropdown chips, and checklists, styled with clean underline fields and subtle row shading.
intro: You can turn a plain Google Doc into a functional fillable form without any add-ons, using nothing but tables, dropdown chips, and checklists. This guide walks through structuring the grid, embedding interactive fields, and cleaning up borders so it looks like a real intake sheet or application.

steps.heading: Build your table structure
steps.body: Use the native Table tool to create organized grid boxes for each user data field you need.

steps.heading: Add dropdown chips for options
steps.body: Embed Dropdown Chips inside form fields to give respondents a clean list of choices to select from.

steps.heading: Insert checklists for multi-option rows
steps.body: Use the Checklist and smart chip utilities from the Insert menu to handle rows with multiple selectable choices.

steps.heading: Turn borders into underline prompts
steps.body: Set specific table border lines to zero points so labeled fields read as clean underline prompts rather than boxed cells.

steps.heading: Shade input areas and lock spacing
steps.body: Apply background cell fills to separate input areas from static labels, and check table properties so the form doesn't shift across pages.

faq.question: Can I add dropdown selections to a Google Docs form?
faq.answer: Yes, Dropdown Chips let you embed selectable menus directly inside table cells.

faq.question: How is this different from a Google Form?
faq.answer: This creates a printable or document-style fillable layout inside Docs itself, rather than a submission-collecting web form.

faq.question: How do I remove the visible table grid lines?
faq.answer: Set the border line width to zero points in table properties to leave a clean, invisible layout.

faq.question: Will my form break if I add more rows later?
faq.answer: Managing table properties and spacing settings helps the structure stay intact as you add or remove fields.
